designChallenge: Select order processing mechanism: direct DB write vs. message queue vs. event sourcing

tradeStudy.title: Order Processing Architecture Selection
tradeStudy.objective: Choose a durable, scalable mechanism for capturing customer orders with at-least-once delivery guarantee

alternatives.name: Direct DB Write
alternatives.description: Synchronous INSERT to PostgreSQL orders table within the HTTP request
cons: DB becomes bottleneck under spike load

alternatives.name: Message Queue (SQS)
alternatives.description: HTTP handler publishes order event to SQS; separate consumer writes to DB

criteria.name: Operational Simplicity
criteria.description: Fewer moving parts, easier incident response

criteria.name: Reliability / At-Least-Once Delivery
criteria.description: Orders must never be silently lost

criteria.name: Cost / Infrastructure Burden
criteria.description: Licensing plus ops effort for a small team

scores.rationale: Single service, single database — every engineer already understands it
ACID prevents corruption but DB saturation under spike causes timeouts without backpressure
No additional infrastructure — existing RDS instance covers this workload
Queue plus consumer adds operational surface area and runbook complexity
SQS at-least-once delivery with idempotency key on order_id prevents silent loss
SQS pricing negligible; consumer ECS task adds ~$40/month plus oncall burden

tradeStudy.justification: Message queue scores 7.05 vs. 7.95 for direct DB — the DB edge on simplicity and cost is outweighed by the reliability gap. Under Black Friday spike load the direct DB path has no backpressure, making cascading timeouts the dominant risk. The queue provides natural load leveling.
tradeStudy.sensitivityNotes: If the team shrinks below 3 engineers and simplicity weight rises to 0.50, the direct DB option pulls ahead. Re-evaluate if peak TPS drops below 500.

keyRisks: Consumer idempotency must be implemented correctly — duplicate order processing is a business risk
Message visibility timeout must exceed max consumer processing time
nextSteps: Prototype SQS consumer with idempotency key on order_id
Load test consumer at 5x peak order rate before production rollout
openIssues: Exact peak TPS estimate not yet measured — current assumption is 2000 TPS based on last Black Friday
SQS vs. Kafka decision deferred pending data retention requirements review
